<!-- Generated by `npm run docs:tools`. Do not edit by hand. -->

# Tool reference

Every tool the server registers, grouped by domain in registration order. Generated from the tool descriptors, so it always matches what ships. Regenerate with `npm run docs:tools`.

## Bridge & versions

| Tool                | Description                                                                                                          | Parameters                                                                          |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `bridge_status`     | Report the editor bridge state: connection, handshake data (Godot/addon versions, project path), and op queue depth. | _none_                                                                              |
| `get_godot_version` | Report the connected editor's Godot version plus the bridge addon and MCP server versions.                           | _none_                                                                              |
| `get_bridge_log`    | Return recent bridge traffic (frames and connection events) for diagnosing editor-bridge issues.                     | `lines` number, optional — How many recent entries to return (default 50, max 200). |

## Onboarding

| Tool             | Description                                                                                                   | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `create_project` | Scaffold a new Godot 4 project (project.godot, icon, main scene, standard folders) in an empty or new folder. | `project_path` string, required — Absolute path to an empty or not-yet-existing folder for the new Godot project.<br>`project_name` string, optional — Human-readable project name; defaults to the target folder name.<br>`godot_version` string, optional — Godot minor to tag config/features with (e.g. "4.4"); defaults to a safe floor. |
| `install_addon`  | Install or update the Godot MCP bridge addon into an existing project, then report the enable steps.          | `project_path` string, required — Absolute path to an existing Godot project directory (containing project.godot).                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

## Project & filesystem

| Tool               | Description                                                                                                                                                                  | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `list_projects`    | Find Godot projects (folders with project.godot) under a directory; depth-capped, skips hidden and system folders.                                                           | `directory` string, required — Absolute path to the directory to search for Godot projects.<br>`recursive` boolean, optional — Recurse into subdirectories (default true).<br>`max_depth` number, optional — Maximum recursion depth (default 5). |
| `get_project_info` | Report the connected project's name, engine version/features, main scene, autoloads, and file counts.                                                                        | _none_                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `list_resources`   | List the connected project's resource files (res:// path, type, uid), optionally filtered by type or directory.                                                              | `type` string, optional — Resource type to filter by, e.g. "PackedScene" or "GDScript".<br>`directory` string, optional — res:// directory prefix to restrict the listing, e.g. "res://scenes".                                                   |
| `import_assets`    | Import new or changed assets into usable res:// resources: pass paths to (re)import specific files, or omit them to rescan the whole project (waits for the scan to finish). | `paths` array, optional — Specific res:// files to (re)import; omit to rescan the whole project for changes.                                                                                                                                      |

## Resource UIDs

| Tool                  | Description                                                                                                                                                | Parameters                                                                                                    |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `get_uid`             | Return the uid:// UID for a res:// resource path (Godot >= 4.4); if the resource has no UID yet, run update_project_uids first.                            | `file_path` string, required — Resource path, e.g. "res://scenes/main.tscn" (project-relative also accepted). |
| `update_project_uids` | Resave every .tscn/.tres lacking a UID inside the editor so all resources carry uid:// IDs (Godot >= 4.4); reports touched, already-had, and failed paths. | _none_                                                                                                        |

## Scenes

| Tool                  | Description                                                                                                                                        | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `create_scene`        | Create a .tscn scene with a chosen root node type and open it in the editor; refuses to overwrite an existing scene.                               | `scene_path` string, required — Project path for the new scene, e.g. "res://scenes/main.tscn" or "scenes/main.tscn".<br>`root_node_type` string, optional — Root node class (default "Node"), e.g. "Node2D", "Node3D", "Control".                                                                                           |
| `open_scene`          | Open (or focus) a scene tab in the editor and make it the current scene that authoring ops target.                                                 | `scene_path` string, required — Project path of the scene to open, e.g. "res://scenes/main.tscn".                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `get_open_scenes`     | List the editor's open scene tabs with each scene's unsaved (dirty) flag, and which scene is current.                                              | _none_                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `save_scene`          | Save the current scene, a named scene, a save-as copy (new_path), or all open scenes; reports what was saved.                                      | `scene_path` string, optional — Scene to save; defaults to the current scene. Must be open.<br>`new_path` string, optional — Save-as target path; saves the current/named scene to this new res:// path.<br>`all` boolean, optional — Save every open scene (default false).                                                |
| `close_scene`         | Close a scene tab (default: the current scene); refuses to discard unsaved changes unless discard is true.                                         | `scene_path` string, optional — Scene to close; defaults to the current scene. Must be open.<br>`discard` boolean, optional — Close even with unsaved changes, discarding them (default false).                                                                                                                             |
| `get_scene_tree`      | Read the current edited scene's live node tree (unsaved state included): each node's type, path, attached script, and instanced-scene marker.      | _none_                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `export_mesh_library` | Export a scene's MeshInstance3D meshes as a MeshLibrary .res resource (for GridMap), optionally filtered by node name; overwrites the output file. | `scene_path` string, required — Scene to export from, e.g. "res://scenes/meshes.tscn".<br>`output_path` string, required — Output resource path, e.g. "res://libraries/meshes.res"; overwritten if present.<br>`mesh_item_names` array, optional — Only export mesh items with these node names; omit to export every mesh. |

## Nodes

| Tool             | Description                                                                                                                                                       | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `add_node`       | Add a node of a given type under a parent in the current scene; registered with the editor's undo so Ctrl+Z reverts it.                                           | `node_type` string, required — Node class to instantiate, e.g. "Node2D", "Sprite2D", "Control".<br>`parent_path` string, optional — Parent node path relative to the scene root (default "." = the root), e.g. "Player".<br>`node_name` string, optional — Name for the new node; defaults to its type name.                                                                                                                                                         |
| `remove_node`    | Remove a node and its whole subtree from the current scene; the response manifests everything removed (names, types, paths), and the editor's Ctrl+Z restores it. | `node_path` string, required — Node to remove, as a path relative to the scene root, e.g. "Player/Sword".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `duplicate_node` | Duplicate a node and its subtree in place as a sibling copy with a unique name, optionally renamed; the editor's Ctrl+Z removes the copy.                         | `node_path` string, required — Node to duplicate, as a path relative to the scene root, e.g. "Enemies".<br>`new_name` string, optional — Name for the copy; defaults to the source name plus a unique suffix.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `move_node`      | Reparent and/or reorder a node in the current scene; reports its new path and how its transform was handled. The editor's Ctrl+Z reverts it.                      | `node_path` string, required — Node to move, as a path relative to the scene root, e.g. "Player/Sword".<br>`new_parent_path` string, optional — New parent node path ("." = the scene root); omit to reorder in place.<br>`index` number, optional — Child position under the (new) parent; omit to append at the end.<br>`keep_global_transform` boolean, optional — On reparent, keep the node's global transform (default true); false keeps its local transform. |
| `rename_node`    | Rename a node in the current scene and get its new path back - every path into that subtree changes. The editor's Ctrl+Z reverts it.                              | `node_path` string, required — Node to rename, as a path relative to the scene root, e.g. "Player/Sword".<br>`new_name` string, required — New node name; sibling collisions get a unique suffix automatically.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

## Properties

| Tool                   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                   | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `read_node_properties` | Read a node's properties in the current scene: by default its non-default (stored) state, or pass properties to fetch specific values. Non-primitive values use Godot text forms.             | `node_path` string, required — Node to read, as a path relative to the scene root, e.g. "Player/Sprite".<br>`properties` array, optional — Specific property names to fetch (stored or not). Omit for the node's non-default state.                                                                                                           |
| `set_node_properties`  | Set one or more properties on a node in the current scene. Godot text forms like "Vector2(100, 50)" are decoded; a res:// path loads into resource-typed properties. Ctrl+Z undoes the batch. | `node_path` string, required — Node to modify, as a path relative to the scene root, e.g. "Player/Sprite".<br>`properties` object, required — Property name -> value. JSON primitives travel natively; other types as Godot text forms; a res:// path loads a resource (resource-typed properties only); null clears Object-typed properties. |

## Diagnostics

| Tool                | Description                                                                                                                                                 | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `get_script_errors` | Parse/compile diagnostics for one GDScript or every .gd in the project, as structured file/line/message/severity records from the editor's language server. | `script_path` string, optional — Project path of a single .gd script to check, e.g. "res://scripts/player.gd" or "scripts/player.gd". Omit to check every GDScript in the project. |

## Run & debug

| Tool               | Description                                                                                                                                                             | Parameters                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `run_project`      | Play the project from the editor: the main scene by default, the current scene (mode: current), or a named scene (scene_path). Output is captured for get_debug_output. | `mode` enum(main \| current \| custom), optional — What to play: "main" (default) = the project main scene, "current" = the scene open in the editor, "custom" = the scene named by scene_path.<br>`scene_path` string, optional — Scene to play, e.g. "res://scenes/main.tscn". Passing it alone implies mode "custom". |
| `stop_project`     | Stop the running game session started from the editor. Safe to call when nothing is playing: returns was_running: false as a structured no-op.                          | _none_                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `get_debug_output` | Tail the running (or last) game session's captured output from the editor's bounded ring buffer; pass the previous call's next_cursor to read only new lines.           | `cursor` number, optional — Return lines after this cursor (omit or 0 = oldest retained). Use next_cursor from the previous call; repeat until lines comes back empty.                                                                                                                                                   |
